title: Comforting Ukrainian Bread Pilaf
description: A sweet and dense one-pot Ukrainian dish featuring bread flour, perfect for a cold winter night. This low-sodium-friendly recipe is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine the bread flour, water, milk, and unsalted butter, and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Add the egg, granulated sugar, active dry yeast, and salt to the saucepan, and stir until the dough comes together in a ball.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Return the dough to the saucepan, cover it with a lid, and let it rise in a warm place for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough, and shape it into a round loaf.
Place the loaf in the saucepan, cover it with a lid, and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the lid, and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, until the bread is golden brown.
Remove the saucepan from the oven, and let the bread cool for 10 minutes before serving.
